title: The computer was already there
caption: Dad bought an Amstrad CPC 464 the year after Matt was born. Computing wasn't a later hobby. It was just part of the house.

title: BASIC before most kids could write properly
caption: Inspired by an older brother learning BASIC, Matt started writing tiny programs absurdly young. By four or five he was already making little things on the Amstrad.

title: The expensive upgrade
caption: Dad bought an IBM 486SX with Windows 3.11. The jump from BASIC to GUI did not feel natural, so DOS remained the comfortable place to tinker.

title: Doom, CD-ROMs and the point of no return
caption: Doom 2, a CD-ROM drive and a faster PC turned games into raw material. They were there to be opened up, edited and improved.

title: The dial-up jar
caption: The family got online through a 28.8k modem. Internet time was rationed and paid for out of pocket money, which made every minute count.

title: Editing worlds
caption: Downloading Doom WADs and editing Wolfenstein files turned playing into making. This is the clearest origin point for later product and systems thinking.

title: First websites
caption: Matt's first website arrived in the mid-90s. From then on there was almost always some kind of web presence, experiment or project online.

title: ICQ, mIRC and the dangerous bits of the web
caption: Online communities taught programming, systems, security and how flimsy most computing really was. Slightly feral education, but useful.

title: Beyond BASIC
caption: Interest widened into early C and Python and game-like experiments. The pattern was already clear: build, break, inspect, rebuild.

title: Freelance before the job title looked serious
caption: Before a conventional career path, Matt worked self-employed building websites, managing hosting, clients and delivery end to end.

title: Snap Media
caption: Copywriting, SEO, video, editing and development in a fast-moving consumer environment. Everything at once, which turned out to be good training.

title: Through the door at the BBC
caption: Entered through content and production, but moved steadily toward technical product and delivery leadership. The BBC was the kind of place that let you drift toward your actual strengths.

title: BBC North Star Award
caption: Personal excellence award in Olympic year, where Matt headed the marketing for London 2012. The kind of recognition that means more when you know what it took.

title: BBC Sport Connected TV App
caption: The Connected TV App that brought BBC Sport to smart TVs. Formula 1, football, Olympics coverage — built for the biggest screen in the house.

title: Technical Product Manager
caption: A formal move into product and technical leadership at the BBC, working on Red Button products and connected services.

title: Big events, bigger systems
caption: iPlayer, BBC Sport TV App, Red Button — and major broadcast events. Olympics, World Cup, Wimbledon, Glastonbury. When things go wrong on those scales, everyone notices.

title: TV App of the Year
caption: Content Innovation Awards, Cannes. BBC Sport TV App. The work had been good for a while — this was just when a room full of people agreed.

title: Consulting and banking at scale
caption: BJSS leadership and Starling Bank product work on business banking, SEPA Instant and direct debit origination. Fintech moved fast and expected you to keep up.

title: Covid platform work
caption: Critical cloud hosting and collaboration infrastructure for the UK Covid-19 response at national scale. The kind of brief you don't really have time to think about before you start.

title: Turning strategy into architecture
caption: At NHSX and NHS England, high-level digital strategy became funded delivery. One architecture document became a £30m NHS App programme.

title: Head of Product, national appointments
caption: Led the design and development of an aggregated system for patient appointments and referrals across NHS App and NHS.uk. Used by millions of people who never thought about how it worked.

title: Health Call CPTO
caption: Patient engagement, notifications, integration engine and virtual wards, including NHS App integration. Proper end-to-end product and technology ownership.

title: First used GPT-2
caption: The moment AI became personal and practical rather than theoretical. Some tools change your thinking about what's possible. This was one of them.

title: Government AI systems
caption: Built secure LLM-enabled systems for the Ministry of Justice. Chatbot capability and dynamic data dashboards against live government datasets. Security-first, no shortcuts.

title: CPTO for Digital Diagnostics
caption: Setting strategic direction for NHS England's Digital Diagnostics and Therapeutics directorate. Securing funding for major programmes and leading product and technical quality at directorate level.
